title: Rick-and-Morty Brainstorming — Gods Above Gods, Give-Then-Take
html:
TL;DR

  • A two-image cosmology fragment: "Gods only being the gods for their specific world, but they all respond and are under a bigger god, who maybe himself has other gods" — fractal-divinity, alternate-universes brain.
  • Visual reference: the cartoon claw-machine picking up a "prize item" — the image mocks the importance of any specific god while still leaving a bottom-most ultimate being.
  • Tagged in his head as "Rick and Morty type of brainstorming thinking" — Yahir is using the show's nested-realities device as a thought primitive.
  • Closes with a behavioral aphorism: "Be different by always giving, then taking." Inversion of the standard sequence — give-first as the alpha move. Reads as a self-instruction.
  • Captured Oct 11, 2021 — OSU freshman fall, two weeks after the "we are all wanting to fit in" note and four days before the JCEF refunds + mentor handoff.

Key quotes

"Gods only being the gods for their specific world, but they all respond and are under a bigger god, who maybe himself has other gods."

"Be different by always giving, then taking."

Open questions

  • Did the give-then-take maxim ever cash out as an actual behavioral pattern? It echoes debt of gratitude and the Will mentor handoff move four days later.
  • The claw-machine image — does this recur? Dream-image inventory worth checking.

Connections
